tasks.description: Tools canvas React Flow UI — replace the flat table tools-view.tsx with an interactive React Flow canvas showing tool nodes (card with name, status badge, cost/mo) and tool section container nodes (resizable groups like process sections). Add 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section' toolbar buttons. Drag tools into/out of tool sections. When nothing selected, show right sidebar summary (total monthly cost, annual cost, tool counts by status). Zoom/pan via React Flow Controls. Keep the existing tools-view.tsx as a fallback reference but create a new tools-canvas-view.tsx as the primary view.
acceptance_criteria: Tools page renders a React Flow canvas (not the old table view)
Tool nodes display: name, status badge (active=green, considering=yellow, cancelled=gray), cost/mo
Tool section nodes are resizable containers (like process section nodes, using NodeResizer)
Toolbar has 'Add Tool' and 'Add Tool Section' buttons (top-left Panel, same pattern as flow-canvas.tsx)
Tools can be dragged — position persists via updateTool API (position_x, position_y)
Tool sections can be dragged/resized — persists via updateToolSection API
Tools inside a tool section use parentId for grouping (same pattern as steps in sections)
Zoom/pan controls (React Flow Controls + MiniMap + Background dots)
Summary sidebar when nothing selected: total monthly cost, annual cost (×12), counts by status (active/considering/cancelled)
Empty state overlay when no tools exist (same pattern as flow-canvas.tsx)
page.tsx fetches both tools AND tool_sections and passes them to the canvas view

similar_files: src/components/canvas/flow-canvas.tsx — primary reference for canvas setup, toolbar, node types, buildNodes/buildEdges pattern
src/components/canvas/section-node.tsx — reference for container/group node with NodeResizer
src/components/canvas/step-node.tsx — reference for item node with badges and handles
src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/[tabId]/canvas-view.tsx — reference for canvas view wrapper with selection state
patterns_to_follow: nodeTypes object with React.memo'd components at module scope
buildNodes() constructs Node[] from DB records, with section nodes having style.width/height and item nodes having parentId for grouping
Tool sections: id prefix 'tool-section-', tools: id prefix 'tool-' (avoids collision with process canvas prefixes)
useNodesState + useEdgesState for React Flow state, sync from props via useEffect
handleNodesChange callback persists position on drag end (change.type === 'position' && change.dragging === false)
Background variant=Dots, Controls showInteractive=false, MiniMap with nodeColor callback
Empty state overlay: absolute inset-0 z-10 pointer-events-none container with pointer-events-auto card
Status badge colors: active=green, considering=yellow, cancelled=gray (like step status pattern in badge.tsx)
learnings_relevant: React 19 useRef: useRef(null) not bare useRef()
Never sort prop arrays directly — use [...arr].sort()
apiFetch unwraps envelope — return apiFetch<T>() directly

tasks.special_instructions: 1. Create tools-canvas-view.tsx as the new primary view component. The old tools-view.tsx should be kept but no longer imported from page.tsx — page.tsx switches to tools-canvas-view.tsx. 2. page.tsx must fetch tool_sections in addition to tools: add a second Supabase query for tool_sections. 3. Tool nodes do NOT have connection handles (no edges between tools — this is a landscape/grouping canvas, not a flow). 4. Tools have tool_section_id field in the DB but this is NOT on the Tool type yet — tools inside sections can be identified by checking if tool.position is inside a section's bounds, OR we add a tool_section_id concept. For simplicity: tools that are inside a tool section use parentId = 'tool-section-{sectionId}' based on spatial containment check on drag end. Wait — actually, looking at the migration, there's no tool_section_id on tools. So grouping is purely visual via React Flow parentId set on drag-drop. Persist this by checking containment: when a tool is dropped, check if its position falls within any tool section's bounds and set the parent accordingly. 5. Summary sidebar: render OUTSIDE the ReactFlow when no tool or section is selected — right-aligned absolute panel. 6. Do NOT add connections/edges — this canvas has no edges.

tasks.description: Gap analysis 'Generate Summary' disabled state guidance — add inline hint directing users to the canvas to set maturity scores when the button is disabled
acceptance_criteria: Disabled 'Generate Summary' button has visible guidance text below or beside it directing users to set maturity scores on steps
Guidance includes a navigable link to the first process canvas tab
When hasGapData is true, guidance text is hidden and button is fully functional

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/ai-analysis/ai-analysis-view.tsx — reference for disabled state patterns with guidance text
patterns_to_follow: Use useWorkspace() to get tabs array, find first process tab for link URL
Link as inline <a> or Next.js Link to /w/{workspaceId}/{firstProcessTabId}

tasks.special_instructions: gap-analysis-view.tsx already has the disabled button with 'Score steps to enable AI narrative.' text. Enhance this by adding a small actionable link below it. Use useWorkspace() to get tabs, find the first tab (or first process tab) and link to it. Pattern: 'Open a step on the canvas to set maturity scores →' linking to /w/{workspaceId}/{tabId}.

tasks.description: Workspace card date hydration fix — replace toLocaleDateString() with toISOString().slice(0,10) on workspace list card dates to prevent SSR/client hydration mismatch
acceptance_criteria: Workspace list card shows creation date in YYYY-MM-DD format (consistent server/client)
No hydration mismatch warnings in browser console

similar_files: src/app/(app)/w/[workspaceId]/ai-analysis/ai-analysis-view.tsx — BUG-030 fix used same pattern: toISOString().slice(0, 10)
patterns_to_follow: Same fix as BUG-030 — consistent hydration-safe date formatting
learnings_relevant: BUG-030 root cause: toLocaleDateString() produces different output on server vs client. toISOString().slice(0, 10) is deterministic.

tasks.special_instructions: Find the toLocaleDateString() call in workspace-list.tsx and replace with new Date(date).toISOString().slice(0, 10). There may be multiple date displays — fix all of them.

shared_files.note: types/canvas.ts is owned by slot 1 only. database.ts is read-only for all slots.
